title: Commercial Analyst – Analytics & Market Intelligence
description:
Requisition ID: R000068593 Commercial Analyst – Analytics & Market Intelligence
Job Location: Singapore – Duo Tower
To submit application, please click below posting link or copy and paste to a new browser: https://chevron.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com/jobs/job/Singapore-DUO-Tower-Singapore/Commercial-Analyst---Analytics---Market-Intelligence_R000068593

Job summary:

Chevron is seeking a highly motivated and analytical Commercial Analyst to join our Trading Analytics team. This role is instrumental in supporting Chevron’s global trading operations by analysing market fundamentals, trade flows, competitor activity, and industry trends. The successful candidate will contribute to value creation by delivering actionable insights and supporting strategic trading decisions.

Responsibilities for this position may include but are not limited to: 

  • Monitor and evaluate market fundamentals, pricing structures, global trade flows, and competitor production profiles and other industry information to generate insights that support trading strategies.

  • Lead and facilitate discussions on fundamentals with a broader team of traders and other analysts, providing timely key market data and relevant analysis to help the Trading team to formulate view of market directions and inform new trade ideas on physical and financial trading.

  • Collaborate with Commercial Analysts and Traders across Houston, Singapore, London and Middle East to support global arbitrage, supply/demand modelling and commercial opportunity identification.

  • Develop and apply models/tools to monitor and forecast key market drivers such as infrastructure, supply/demand, and industry production to support trading strategies.

  • Identify and recommend trading opportunities using deterministic analytical models and dynamic analytical models, as well as apply quantitative risk management methodologies to guide trading teams in informed decision-making.

  • Model deal economics/ analysis to identify feasible alternatives with trade-offs.

  • Pilot and leverage advanced digital tools-including machine learning and predictive analytics-to enhance trading performance and uncover new market opportunities.

  • Conduct performance lookbacks on trading outcomes and assess the accuracy of market calls and price forecasts.

  • Build and maintain a network of industry contacts to enhance market intelligence, generate insights and conduct economic analysis for future opportunities.

  • Champion process improvements by partnering with internal teams to optimize digital tools and pursue analytical process improvement opportunities to reduce manual effort.

Required Qualifications: 

  • Bachelor's degree with a minimum of 3 years of experience in Oil & Gas Industry, trading, market data analytics, or related roles.

  • Solid understanding of commodity markets, including crude, natural gas, renewables, and/or refined products. Experience analysing commodity market fundamentals, with a preference for that to have been in NGL and Petrochemical, LNG, gas or power markets would be advantageous.

  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills. Demonstrated ability to manipulate and analyse data sets and use economic models to test sensitivities and identify key value drivers all with minimal direction.

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, Power BI, and PowerPoint; experience with Python for data analysis and visualization is preferred.

  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to present market insights clearly and concisely to influence and achieve optimum decisions.

  • A collaborative team player who thrives in a fast-paced environment and ability to work effectively in a cross-functional team environment.

  • A digital-first mindset with a passion for innovation, ability to utilize decision analysis tools and processes to evaluate multiple commercial options and drive continuous improvement.

postedCompany.description:
Singapore is the regional headquarters for chevron's downstream operations in the asia-pacific region.


Chevron has a significant presence in Singapore, dating back to 1933. The company employs around 1,100 people in the country and are involved in several key areas:


Regional Headquarters. Singapore is the regional headquarters for Chevron’s downstream operations in Asia-Pacific including manufacturing, marketing, and lubricants businesses.


Caltex Brand Management. The Caltex brand, which serves customers in Asia and the Middle East, is managed from Singapore.


Refining. Chevron has a 50 percent interest in the Singapore Refining Company (SRC).


The SRC refinery processes up to 290,000 barrels of crude oil per day, producing liquefied petroleum gas, gasoline, jet fuel, diesel, fuel oil and asphalt. Recent upgrades have enabled the refinery to produce higher-value gasoline that meets stricter emission standards.


Lubricant Additives. Chevron Oronite’s regional headquarters for Asia Pacific and home to one of the largest fuel and lubricant additives manufacturing facilities in Asia is in Singapore.


Shipping and Storage. Chevron has a key presence in Singapore’s vital shipping industry with a blending plant and a terminal with over 400,000 cubic meters of storage capacity.


Trading. Chevron’s Supply & Trading organization provides commercial support to our crude oil and natural gas production operations and to our refining and marketing network and has a regional trading hub in Singapore that partners with our headquarters in Houston, Texas and other trading hubs in London, UK and San Ramon, California.


Chevron New Energies (CNE). Is the arm of Chevron focused on developing lower carbon solutions in the Asia Pacific region.  CNE established its headquarters in Singapore 2021 and aims to help customers meet their lower carbon goals which includes carbon capture utilization and storage, hydrogen, and renewable fuels.


In Singapore, Chevron hires full-time employees with relevant education and experience in the following areas:


• Asset Commercialization

• Business Development

• Category Management

• Procurement

• Finance

• Information Technology/Data Analytics

• Shipping

• Supply & Trading


What We Offer:


• Comprehensive and competitive remuneration that are made up of short and long-term components.

• Hybrid, Flexible work-life balance options

• Ongoing learning and career development
postedCompany.name: CHEVRON SINGAPORE PTE. LTD.
